name: Modular construction standards — ICC/MBI 1200 + 1205 / CSA A277 / NEN 9090 / DfMA
standard: ICC/MBI 1200-2021 (Off-site construction — Planning, design, fabrication and assembly) + ICC/MBI 1205-2021 (Inspection and regulatory compliance) + CSA A277:2016 (Procedure for factory certification of buildings) + NEN 9090 (Netherlands — prefab dimensional coordination) + Buildoffsite DfMA principles (UK)
description: Enables OCERP built-in validation rules covering the procedural standards for off-site / modular construction in the major regulated markets. ICC/MBI 1200 sets fabrication and assembly requirements (US); ICC/MBI 1205 covers third-party inspection and AHJ regulatory compliance; CSA A277 is the Canadian factory-certification procedure (mandatory in most provinces for non-site-built buildings); NEN 9090 governs dimensional coordination for prefab in the Netherlands; Buildoffsite DfMA (Design for Manufacture and Assembly) principles cover the 7-step DfMA score (standardisation, modularisation, integration, simplification, repeatability, factory tolerance, lift/transport optimisation).
